TeresaSweeney

Author, and host of Rewritten. Speaking and writing for ambitious working women who love their careers, love their families, and are done believing they have to choose between the two.

Subscribe Listen to Rewritten
McKinsey→ Private equity→ GLG→ Blackstone→ Head of Investment Intelligence, NY investment firm
Meet Teresa

Author, Wall Street Executive, Podcast Host, and Mother of Two.

I've spent 17+ years building a career in finance and strategy — from McKinsey to private equity, GLG, Blackstone, and now as Head of Investment Intelligence at a New York-based investment firm.

For a long time, I thought success meant getting to the next room, the next role, the next milestone.

And then I started to wonder: what if I wanted the career — but not the version where everything else I cared about got squeezed out around it?

That question became the foundation of my work.

I speak and write about ambition, motherhood, leadership, health, marriage, boundaries, and how we can avoid chasing definitions of success that don't fit, and that we never consciously chose. My work is for women who want meaningful careers and full lives — and who refuse to believe they have to choose between the two.

I live in New York with my husband and two kids.

Rewritten

My Podcast

Real conversations for ambitious women rewriting the rules around work, marriage, and motherhood. New episodes out now.

On Your Terms

My Newsletter · on Substack

For the days you want more than a scroll. My writing in full, in your inbox, before it lands anywhere else.

The Book

Coming Fall 2027

For the woman who has done everything she was supposed to do — and still feels like something is missing. This book is about redefining success, protecting what matters, and building a life on your own terms. Join the email list to hear about it first!

Writing for ambitious women done living someone else's script.

Everyone wants to know whether women can "have it all." I think that's the wrong question. There's no one "all." There's only the version you choose — or the version you drift into because you never stopped long enough to define it. This is for ambitious women who want serious careers, full lives, and a definition of success that doesn't require sacrificing everything else they care about.

What I write about
Where it begins

The Wake-Up

The moment we realize we may be winning at a game we never consciously chose. Stories about ambition, motherhood, career pivots, "made it" moments, and the question underneath all of it: Is this actually what I want?

Explore →
The honest part

The Reckoning

What we accept becomes the life we live. An honest look at the standards we've inherited, the patterns we've tolerated, and the places we've negotiated against ourselves.

Explore →
The daily work

The Practice

Living on our own terms isn't one bold decision. It's the small, daily choices that move us toward who we want to be.

Explore →
A new book by Teresa SweeneyTitle
revealed
soonCover · coming

My book will be published Fall 2027. The title's still under wraps.

A playbook for ambitious women ready to stop living by someone else's definition of success — and start building their own.

  • Define what you actually want — not what you inherited.
  • Raise the standard for what you accept at work, at home, and in your own head.
  • Protect the time, energy, and relationships that make the rest of your life work.
  • Build one framework across career, self, partnership, and parenting.
Be first to know Title, cover, and launch date announced to the list first.
My Podcast

Rewritten

Co-hosted with my longtime friend Jess Wass, Rewritten is for ambitious women in demanding careers grappling with the gap between the life that looks right on paper and the one that actually feels like theirs. Real conversations for women rewriting what it means to be a mom, a wife, and a boss.

Core beliefs
01There's no one "all." You define it for yourself.
02Your life is shaped less by what you want than by what you accept.
03The small, daily choices become the pattern.
04Discomfort is a compass, not a warning.
05You don't need the whole story — just the next line.
06A career and a life are one system, not two.
